06035.name: Lassen
06035.description: The LASSEN COUNTY CLERK’S OFFICE has a new policy: they will no longer release or confirm dates of birth (DOB) or other personally identifiable information (PII) for potential cases, and all court copies will have DOBs and PII redacted. Our teams are working with the court to understand this and find new ways to confirm identifying information. The last three month's LASSEN COUNTY average TAT was 17.65 days (66 max. & 0.68 min.).

06037.name: Los Angeles
06037.description:
Effective February 23, 2024, LOS ANGELES COUNTY removed "partial birthdate" from researcher access terminals, impacting criminal record processing. Initially, non-criminal records were quicker, but now all criminal searches need clerk assistance. The county has since removed all identifying information, requiring clerk searches and hard copy file verification for potential criminal records. Clerks are also limiting daily searches.
Despite this, our research partners have developed new submission systems and hired additional researchers to increase search capacity, mitigating the per-day limits. While delays are still expected, overall TAT’s have improved. The last three month's LOS ANGELES COUNTY average TAT was 0.88 days (28.06 max. & 0.0006 min.).
